description: Declared bindings from NOOS classification routes (from the observability-semantics change) to Motor recipes. This is a DECLARATION of intent, not a live wiring: status=NOT_WIRED means NOOS classifies and routes correctly but no registered machine owner executes the recipe yet. Correctly stopping without repair is safer than incorrectly restarting production.
not_wired_is_safe: A route with no machine_owner surfaces the finding and stops. It never falls back to execution restart.

bindings.note: Track D wired 2026-07-17: the owner consumes a DISPATCHING_COMPLETION_UNPROVEN diagnosis, performs ONLY keyed idempotent retries of failed evidence-sink writes (read-before-write on run_id, L13), proves recovery solely via the before/after external check pair through the observer read path, and ESCALATES to a founder-decision issue when no retryable outbox exists — it contains no execution-dispatch or restart vocabulary (test-enforced in tests/test_noos_receipt_writer_repair_v1.py). Scheduling onto trigger hosts / the CF loop fleet remains founder-gated (frozen data/ registries + deploy gate); until then NOOS consumers and operators invoke it on route findings.
Handled today by the existing reconcile/self-heal loop; not yet a registered Motor recipe/job.
Divergence between dispatch and completion observers routes to a diagnosis, not a repair.
Authoritative source unqueryable — diagnose the monitoring path, do not touch execution.
